body: Nature, Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10624-2 Analysis of a 25-year satellite record shows that Earth has a persistent east–west albedo symmetry split at 27° E, with clear-sky albedo, cloud radiative effect and open-ocean fraction exhibiting a triple symmetry around this meridian.

title: Ubiquitin tags detected on non-protein biomolecules using new method
summary:
Nature, Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01741-z

An innovative method of mass spectrometry has detected the small protein ubiquitin on biomolecules other than proteins. In mouse liver, the energy-storing mo…
body: Nature, Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01741-z An innovative method of mass spectrometry has detected the small protein ubiquitin on biomolecules other than proteins. In mouse liver, the energy-storing molecule glycogen has a large increase in ubiquitination — involving 1% of the liver’s total ubiquitin — during fasting, when glycogen is broken down.

title: Smartphone camera takes users’ pulse passively during device use
summary:
Nature, Published online: 01 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01488-7

A machine-learning system has been developed that can monitor heart rate using facial video clips that are captured passively by the user-facing camera durin…
body: Nature, Published online: 01 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01488-7 A machine-learning system has been developed that can monitor heart rate using facial video clips that are captured passively by the user-facing camera during everyday smartphone use. The system meets industry accuracy standards for heart-rate measurement and is as accurate as wearable technology for measuring daily resting heart rate.
